text: Cancer cases worldwide are expected to soar in the coming decades, a report finds. Here’s why. - The Washington Post

text: Last woman executed in Britain for killing abusive lover is given a conditional pardon - AP News
fullText: Ruth Ellis, the last woman executed in Britain, has been posthumously granted conditional pardon, according to Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy. Ellis, a 28-year-old single mother, was hanged in 1955 for killing her abusive lover, David Blakely. The pardon a…

text: Elaine Chao releases statement amid husband Mitch McConnell's long hospital stay - WLKY
fullText: It's been more than three weeks since Sen. Mitch McConnell was admitted to the hospital, and while questions about his health remain unanswered, we're hearing from the senator's wife for the first time.

text: She blamed flu shots for her twins’ deaths. Now she’s charged with murder. - The Washington Post
fullText: Andrea Shaw, a plaintiff in a lawsuit against the American Academy of Pediatrics for recommending childhood vaccines, is accused of suffocating her daughter and son.

text: Maine Democrats say Platner's campaign is trying to influence replacement process - NPR
fullText: The state Democratic Party accused Platner's campaign of trying to "put their thumb on the scale" of the process to replace him if he drops his bid for Senate. Platner's team has denied doing so.

text: German doctor jailed for killing 15 of his patients - BBC

text: Live updates: Trump says ceasefire ‘over’ after US and Iran trade strikes - CNN

text: 'They Now Wait for This Storm to Pass' — PS5 Owners Are Cancelling PlayStation Plus Subscriptions to Protest Sony Killing Discs, but Analysts Say It Won't Make Any Difference - IGN
fullText: PS5 owners are cancelling PlayStation Plus subscriptions in protest at Sony's controversial decision to stop releasing games on physical discs — but the gesture will not force the console maker to change its mind, analysts say.

text: Michigan Cyclosporiasis outbreak tops 700 cases as health officials search for source - ClickOnDetroit | WDIV Local 4
fullText: More than 700 Michiganders have fallen ill with cyclosporiasis, a severe stomach bug linked to parasites found in food or water, and health officials warn the worst may not be over.
